adds.rationale: Dominant AI compute provider; data-center GPU demand accelerating and the pending $40B Arm acquisition signals a deliberate move to consolidate the AI silicon stack.

news_evidence.summary: NVIDIA announces definitive agreement to acquire Arm from SoftBank for $40B, framed as creating the premier computing company for the age of AI.

adds.rationale: Just-launched actively-managed space-exploration ETF gives diversified, listed exposure to the rockets-and-spacecraft wave without single-name SPAC risk.

news_evidence.summary: ARK Invest launches ARKX, the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF, its first new ETF in two years; targets orbital and suborbital aerospace, enabling tech, and beneficiaries.

adds.rationale: Global X Robotics & AI ETF gives diversified listed exposure to industrial automation, factory robotics, and autonomous-systems names that are the early on-ramp to the robotics wave.

news_evidence.summary: TSMC flags persistent chip-capacity tightness and accelerating demand from automotive and industrial automation customers, underscoring durable demand for the factory-automation names BOTZ holds.

rationale_overall: First quarterly rebalance: keep the entire day-0 watchlist intact and open three new wave-thesis positions. NVDA deepens AI exposure beyond mega-cap software; ARKX opens the rockets-and-spacecraft bucket via a diversified ETF on its first trading day; BOTZ opens the robotics bucket. Watchlist grows from 5 to 8 names, still well under the cap of 12.
